name: The Synapse
subtitle: Where women connect mind, machine, and what comes next
tagline: A women-centered gathering at the frontier of consciousness, AI, cognitive science, and robotics.

fiscalSponsor.name: Applied Love Labs
fiscalSponsor.blurb: The Synapse is hosted by Applied Love Labs, founded by neuroscientist Julia Mossbridge, PhD. Charitable contributions flow through ALL, which issues tax-deductible receipts in its own name.

mission: The Synapse convenes women working at the edges of consciousness research, artificial intelligence, cognitive science, and robotics. A small, invitation-shaped conversation -- 50 to 75 people -- where early-career researchers sit at the same table as senior scholars, and where the work being done is treated as the point, not the performance.

principles.title: Everyone belongs
principles.body: Every person in the room is here on purpose. No one has to prove they belong by performing.

principles.title: Engagement over performance
principles.body: Sparse plenaries. More workshops, facilitated dialogue, art, and structured time for the conversations that usually only happen in hallways.

principles.title: Participants are compensated
principles.body: The ethos of equality starts with what we value enough to fund. Those who lead a session are paid.

principles.title: Women-centered, ally-welcoming
principles.body: Women lead and convene. Anyone who supports the mission is welcome to apply.

principles.title: Small enough to matter
principles.body: 50-75 guests. Deep connection is a design constraint, not a nice-to-have.

placeholderNote: Venue, registration rates, participant roster, and session titles are placeholders -- see CONTENT_PLACEHOLDER.md. Applied Love Labs is confirmed as the host / fiscal sponsor; Atlanta October 9-11, 2026 and the 50-75 capacity are confirmed; the five ethos principles and the four-field framing align with whp.vercel.app, the committee's reference build.
